observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents.

Moving forward, the LCE should ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers are properly authorized and supported with the required documents before its final approval for payment.
observations.reason: Submitted justification for the compliance of lacking documents worth  ₱11,898,084.65; Lacking documents for the purchase of land worth ₱1,050,000.00 not yet submitted. This is a reiterated finding in the CY 2024 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the cost of the Sea Ambulance and its accompanying depreciation. Henceforth, ensure that any donated properties are recorded as soon as they are received.
1.7	We further recommended that the General Services Officer include in the property records the donated sea ambulance.

3.18	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
1.	the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of the transfer of land titles in favor of the Municipality or register the Deed of Absolute Sale in the Registry of Deeds to avoid any future land dispute; and
2.	the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future land acquisitions are properly supported with necessary documents.
3.19	This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to expedite the physical count of properties not included in the RPCPPE, as well as the reconciliation of GSO and accounting records, to establish the correct balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the cost of the donated Patient Transport Vehicle and its accompanying depreciation. Henceforth, ensure that any donated properties are recorded as soon as they are received. 

We further recommended that the General Services Officer include in the property records the donated one unit of PTV.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to correct the deficiency and going forward, always compute the depreciation in accordance with IPSAS 17 and PAG 3 of IPSAS 17.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
1.	prepare necessary adjusting entries, utilization reports, and disclosures; and

2.	to facilitate the remittance/return of any unutilized cash donation to the BoT or the LGU- Baguio City, whichever is applicable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs:

1.	the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of the transfer of land titles in favor of the Municipality or register the Deed of Absolute Sale in the Registry of Deeds to avoid any future land dispute; and
2.	the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future land acquisitions are properly supported with necessary documents.
observations.reason: The processing of land titles in favor of the Municipality is still on-going. This is a reiterated finding in the CY 2024 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly follow the rules and regulations governing the utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory of properties for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances; 2. General Services Office remove the items below the new capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 as PPE and uses the RPCPPE format to report the result of the physical count of PPEs other than the Local Road Network, providing complete information; 3. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to a) regularly update the PPELCs and Property Cards, respectively; and b) reconcile both records and determine the cause/s of the variance for proper disposition thereof.
observations.reason: Consolidated with CY 2023 Findings

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous CY 2023 and CY 2022 recommendation that Management require the: a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2018 and to maintain updated Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular No. 2002-003; and b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular No. 2002-003.
observations.reason: Consolidated with CY 2023 and CY 2022 Findings

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents. Moving forward, the LCE should ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers are properly authorized and supported with the required documents before its final approval for payment.
observations.reason: Lacking supporting documents not yet submitted for the purchase of land amounting to ₱1,050,000.00 while the remaining balance were requested by Management to be complied with for future transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to
expedite the physical count of properties not included in the RPCPPE, as well as the
reconciliation of GSO and accounting records, to establish the correct balance of the
PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the cost of
the donated Patient Transport Vehicle and its accompanying depreciation.
Henceforth, ensure that any donated properties are recorded as soon as they are
received.

We further recommended that the General Services Officer include in the property
records the donated one unit of PTV.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to
correct the deficiency and going forward, always compute the depreciation in
accordance with IPSAS 17 and PAG 3 of IPSAS 17.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
1. prepare necessary adjusting entries, utilization reports, and disclosures; and
2. to facilitate the remittance/return of any unutilized cash donation to the BoT
or the LGU- Baguio City, whichever is applicable.

We recommended that the: 1. Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to remove from the Inventory accounts the items still in-transit as of year-end and those issued as semi-expendable properties; 2. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties; and to segregate semi-expendable properties as low-valued and high-valued items.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant demand for the liquidation of the fund transferred to Barangays in previous years or an update regarding the extent of implementation of the program/project/activity; and 2. Local Chief Executive ensures that the MOA for ensuing fund transfers contains specific provisions for the purpose implementation period and submission of liquidation reports by the implementing units.

We recommended the submission by the Local Chief Executive or the Municipal Engineer of the following documents for further evaluation of the transactions: 1. Justification for the approval of the Time Extension when the reason given must have been considered in the Detailed Engineering, prior to bidding; 2. Statement of Time Elapse; 3. Certification from PAGASA to establish the weather condition causing the unworkable days within the contract period; and 4. Suspension order, resume order and/or change order to support the revised PERT/CPM due to work extension.
observations.reason: Submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Human Resource Management Officer strictly follow the guidelines in hiring JO personnel set forth under CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, and stop hiring JO which performs the work of existing regular employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: 1. Municipal Assessor to prepare an updated Schedule of Fair Market Value (SFMV) and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan. 2. Sangguniang Bayan to enact the prepared SFMV into an ordinance. 3. SB Secretary to submit the newly created ordinance to the Governor’s Office for review in accordance with Section 30, part (a) of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: Justification submitted by Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate immediately his outstanding cash balance in accordance with Section 89 of P. D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Submitted liquidation of the outstanding cash balance amounting to ₱121,000.00;

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU return/refund immediately the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to liquidate the same. Likewise, for the fund amounting to ₱100,000.00, the Accountant should exert effort to identify the source agency, otherwise, return the fund to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to observe due diligence in the conduct of the bidding process especially in the evaluation of the bidding documents and qualification of the bidders at all times.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate immediately his outstanding cash advances in accordance with Section 89 of P. D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management require the: a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2018 and to maintain updated Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular No. 2002-003; b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular No. 2002-003.

This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents. observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the:
1. Municipal Mayor creates an Inventory Committee to complete the physical
inventory of properties for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances;
2. General Services Office remove the items below the new capitalization threshold
of 50,000.00 as PPE and uses the RPCPPE format to report the result of the
physical count of PPEs other than the Local Road Network, providing complete
information; and
3. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to a) regularly update the
PPELCs and Property Cards, respectively; and b) reconcile both records
and determine the cause/s of the variance for proper disposition thereof.

1.8 Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

2.6 We recommended that the:
1. Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to remove from the
Inventory accounts the items still in-transit as of year-end and those issued semi-
expendable properties; and
2. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer prepare and maintain the
prescribed forms, registries and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-
004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties; and to segregate
semi-expendable properties as low-valued and high-valued items.

2.7 Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

3.6 We recommended that the:
1. Municipal Accountant demand for the liquidation of the fund transferred to
Barangays in previous years or an update regarding the extent of implementation
of the program/project/activity; and
2. Local Chief Executive ensures that the MOA for ensuing fund transfers contains
specific provisions for the implementation period and submission of liquidation
reports by the implementing units.

3.7 Management committed to the compliance of the audit recommendations by demanding the
barangays for the immediate submission of liquidation documents.

4.11 We recommended the submission by the Local Chief Executive or the Municipal
Engineer of the following documents for further evaluation of the transactions:
1. Justification for the approval of the Time Extension when the reason given must
have been considered in the Detailed Engineering, prior to bidding;
2. Statement of Time Elapse;
3. Certification from PAGASA to establish the bad weather condition causing the
unworkable days within the contract period; and
4. Suspension order, resume order and/or change order to support the revised
PERT/CPM due to work extension.

4.12 Management committed to submit the lacking documentary requirements and to ensure
compliance with the documentary requirements for all infrastructure projects.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Human Resource Management
Officer strictly follow the guidelines in hiring JO personnel set forth under Section 1 of
CSC-Resolution No. 020790, dated June 5, 2002, and stop hiring JO which performs
the work of existing regular employees.

5.7 Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management collect relevant documents and request authority from COA to write-off the receivables which are non-moving for more than 10 years, after all the remedies have been exhausted, in accordance with Sections 5.4 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to exert effort to gather data on the cost of the road lot component of each of the Local Road Network included in the Road Map of the municipality and commence the establishment of the LGUs ownership on the lots.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Management refrain from procurement of including gasoline and other expenses in the reimbursement of travel to avoid erroneous recording of expenses and to strictly implement the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184).

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording as outright expense the purchased/acquired items but instead record such in their appropriate inventory accounts first and recognize expense only upon issuance to end-users, based on the RIS and SSMI prepared by the General Services or Local Treasurer.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDC to: • Submit the supplemental AIPs related to the PPAs above. And, • Prepare and submit supplemental AIP on all PPAs not included in the supplemental Annual Investment Program and submit thereafter to the audit team.

We recommended that the LCE require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly to the preparation of APP in the implementation of the procurement activities and/or issue a resolution in cases of deviations from the plan.

We recommended the LCE to: a) Direct the Municipal Agriculturist, supported by sufficient staff to establish the data relating to the claim of the Municipality from the debtors relating to the KKK Livelihood Program of the Municipality; b) Establish sufficient evidence to warrant the request for write-off of the account; c) File a request for write-off of the dormant account if proven necessary; and, d) Make necessary adjustments on the account basing on the results of the execution of the recommendation numbers a to c.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDC to:
 Submit the supplemental AIPs related to the PPAs above. And,
 Prepare and submit supplemental AIP on all PPAs not included in the Annual Investment Program, and submit thereafter to the audit team.

We recommended that the LCE require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly to the preparation of APP in the implementation of the procurement activities and/or issue a resolution in cases of deviations from the plan.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to observe due diligence in the conduct of the bidding process especially in the evaluation of the bidding documents and qualification of the bidders at all times.

We recommended the LCE a) Direct the Municipal Agriculturist, supported by sufficient staff to establish the data relating to the claim of the Municipality from the debtors relating to KKK Livelihood Program of the government; b) Establish sufficient evidence to warrant the request for write-off of the account; c) File a request for write-off of the dormant account if proven necessary; and, d) Make necessary adjustments on the account basing on the results of the execution of the recommendation numbers a to c.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Moving forward, the LCE should ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers are properly authorized and supported with the required documents before its final approval for payment.

We recommend that the Management collect relevant documents and request
authority from COA to write-off the receivables which are non-moving for more than
10 years, after all the remedies have been exhausted, in accordance with Sections 5.4
and 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to
exert effort to gather data on the cost of the road lot component of each of the Local
Road Network included in the Road Map of the municipality and commence the
establishment of the LGUs ownership on the lots.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate
immediately his outstanding cash advances in accordance with Section 89 of P. D.
1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the LGU return/refund immediately the unutilized balances
of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate
the same.
Likewise, for the fund amounting to P100,000.00, the Accountant should exert effort
to identify the source agency, otherwise, return the fund to the Bureau of the
Treasury.

We recommended that management refrain from including gasoline and other
expenses in the reimbursement of travel to avoid erroneous recording of expenses and
to strictly implement the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA
9184).

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from
recording as outright expense the purchased/acquired inventories but instead record
such in their appropriate inventory accounts first and recognize expense only upon
issuance, based on the RIS and SSMI prepared by the General Services or Local
Treasurer.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies and furnish the audit team with the copies of the JEV.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification on the mode of procurement employed by the municipality on the purchase of pork from local hog raisers.
Henceforth, strictly observe the modes of procurement provided under RA No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format be immediately prepared with complete supporting documents, submitted, and posted thereafter in at least three (3) conspicuous places.
We recommended that the LCE direct The Municipal Accountant, LDRRMO and the Mun. Budget Officer reconcile their records and submit to the audit team the corrected copies.

We recommended that: 1. LCE, in coordination with the Budget Officer, make proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Budget; and 2. Sangguniang Bayan adhere strictly to existing rules, laws and regulations in the use of appropriated funds and savings.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies and furnish the audit team with the copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification on the mode of procurement employed by the municipality on the purchase of pork from local hog raisers. Henceforth, strictly observe the modes of procurement provided under RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended that the required monthly fund utilization reports and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format (Annex C) be immediately prepared with complete supporting documents, submitted, and posted thereafter in at least three (3) conspicuous places.
We recommended that the LCE direct The Municipal Accountant, LDRRMO and the Mun. Budget Officer to reconcile their records and submit to the audit team the corrected copies.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDC to:
Submit the supplemental AIPs related to the PPAs above. And,
Prepare and submit supplemental AIP on all PPAs not included in the Annual Investment Program and submit thereafter to the audit team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly to the preparation of APP in the implementation of the procurement activities and/or issue a resolution in cases of deviations from the plan.

We recommended that the:
1.	Local Chief Executive (LCE), in coordination with the Budget Officer, make proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Budget; and
2.	Sangguniang Bayan adhere strictly to existing rules, laws and regulations in the use of appropriated funds and savings.

We recommended that management refrain from conducting meetings outside the Municipality when there are available function or venue in the conference halls within the locality or in the nearby cities.
observations.reason: There is no available function or venue in the municipality to accommodate their officials and employees, that will let them perform the purpose of their activity without disturbance by their regular functions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the discussed
deficiencies.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct:
1. The Municipal Assessor to gather data on the cost of the road lot component of
each of the Local Road Network included in the Road Map of the municipality
and commence the establishment of the LGUs ownership on the lots;
2. The Municipal Engineer to establish the cost of each of the road network
components, except road lot; and,
3. The Municipal Accountant to adjust the local road network account basing on the
reconciled results of the data gathered from the Mun. Assessor and Municipal
Engineer.

We recommended and management agreed that the:
1. LCE, in coordination with the MBO to make realistic or doable targets in the
preparation of the Annual Budget by consulting all concerned officials and employees
during such preparations;
2. Sangguniang Bayan to review appropriately the Budget before approving; and,
3. Municipal Treasurer to adopt more effective collection strategies and intensify
collection campaign to meet the collections targets of every year.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to
observe due diligence in the conduct of the bidding process specially in the evaluation of the
bidding documents and qualification of the bidders at all times.

We reiterate our previous recommendation that Management require the: (a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical Report of Inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2018 and to maintain updated Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular 2002-003; (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular 2002-003.
observations.reason: Conduct of inventory is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the Municipal Agriculturist, supported by sufficient staff to establish the data relating to the claim of the Municipality from the debtors relating to the KKK Livelihood Program of the Municipality; b. Establish sufficient evidence to warrant the request for write-off of the dormant account; c. File a request for write-off of the dormant account if proven necessary; and, d. Make necessary adjustments on the account basing on the results of the execution of the recommendation numbers a to c.
observations.managementAction: Already written-off in the Other Receivables account but request for write-off was not yet received by the Office of the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Request for write-off was not yet received by the Office of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of allowing partial liquidation of cash advances for travels of officials and employees in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials and to officials without designation as disbursing officer and without proper bonding in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.

1. Consider prudence in hiring contracting consultancy services considering the necessity and exigency of the required professional expertise guided by the principle of economy to safeguard government resources against wastage; 2. Adhere to relevant provisions of RA No. 9184 in procurement of consultancy services; and, 3. Revisit the organizational structure, staffing pattern and service requirements of the agency and submit justification on the necessity of hiring the consultant for over two (2) years.

Disqualify the participation of the LGU’s own employees’ cooperative in all of the LGUs procurement; Determine the existence of conflict of interest in all procurement activities; and, Observe competitiveness in government procurement at all times.
We recommended that LCE refrain from issuing travel orders to personnel without prior evaluation as to the necessity of their participation and attendance to such meetings.

1. make policy guidelines (within the bounds of the law) for the issuance of communication allowance and ensure its strict compliance thereof to avoid disallowance. 2. direct the municipal accountant to require the attachment of certification that the amount of communication allowance was used for official calls and text messages, and to attach proof of purchase of cell cards, if any from suppliers by including the used cell cards in the claim.

We recommended the Local School Board to conduct regular meetings and follow the specific guidelines of the JMC 1, S. 2017 as stated above to be able to craft and approve budget for the SEF for the current year 2019 and the ensuing years.

We recommended the strict compliance and enforcement of collection of SET by continuous information campaign and annual collection drive to increase available resources to support the needs of schools in the municipality of Sulat.

a. make careful planning of the LDRRMC to cater all projects, programs and activities that are needed for disaster risk reduction and management, and revise the LDRRM Investment Plan, if needed, with proper authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan. b. observe strict use monitoring of the DRRM Funds and proper charging from it.

a. the MDRRMO to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC to this office, and further require the accounting and budget office units to assist in the preparation of the said report to be certified by the Municipal Accountant; b. the MDRRMO and the Municipal accountant to include in the abovementioned report the all available resources for LDRRM use such as unexpended prior year balances, donations or grants received from the national agencies, local government units or foreign sources, if any.

1. MSWDO to review meticulously all budget items included in her office to avoid hidden MOOE items in the 20% Development Fund. 2. Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate only those PPAs in the 20% Development Fund which are allowed per DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that LCE directs the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents. Moving forward, the LCE should ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers are properly authorized and supported with the required documents before its final approval for payment.
observations.reason: There were still lacking documents in various DVs submitted to the team.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the Land, Land Improvements and Buildings be traced to the historical cost and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipal should be supported either with an original or transfer certificate of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: It was already prepared.
observations.reason: This finding is related to physical count of Real Property Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our previous recommendation that Management require the:
(a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical
Inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2017 and to maintain
updated Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular 2002-003;
(b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular
2002-003.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE :
(a) Direct the Municipal Agriculturist, supported by sufficient staff to
establish the data relating to the claim of the Municipality from the debtors relating
to the KKK Livelihood Program of the government;
(b) Establish sufficient evidence to warrant the request for write-off of the
account;
(c) File a request for write-off of the dormant account if proven necessary; and,
(d) Make necessary adjustments on the account basing on the results of the
execution of the recommendation numbers a to c.

We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of allowing
partial liquidation of cash advances for travels of officials employees in compliance
with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.

We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of granting cash advances
to elected officials and to officials without proper designation as disbursing officer and
without proper bonding in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17,
1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.

This is without prejudice to Notices of Disallowance that may be issued, if
warranted.

We recommended and management agreed to:
(a) Consider prudence in contracting consultancy services considering the
necessity and exigency of the required professional expertise guided by the principle
of economy to safeguard government resources against wastage;
(b) Adhere to relevant provisions of RA No. 9184 in procurement of consultancy
services; and,
(c) Revisit the organizational structure, staffing pattern and service requirements of
the agency and submit justification on the necessity of hiring the consultant for over
two (2) years.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to
direct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
(a) Disqualify the participation of the LGU’s own employees’ cooperative in
all of the LGUs procurement;
(b) Determine the existence of conflict of interest in all procurement
activities; and,
(c) Observe competitiveness in government procurement at all times.

We recommended and management agreed to refrain from conducting meetings outside
the Municipality when there are available function or conference halls within the locality
or in the nearby cities.

We recommended and management agreed that LCE refrain from issuing travel orders
to personnel without prior evaluation as to the necessity of their participation and
attendance to such meetings.

We recommended and management agreed to:
(a) )make policy guidelines (within the bounds of the law) for the issuance of
communication allowance and ensure its strict compliance thereof to avoid disallowance.
(b) direct the municipal accountant to require the attachment of certification that the
amount of communication allowance was used for official calls and text messages, and to
attach proof of purchase of cell cards, if any from suppliers by including the used cell
cards in the claim.

We recommended that the Local School Board conduct regular meetings and follow
the specific guidelines of the JMC 1, S. 2017 as stated above to be able to craft and approve
budget for the SEF for the current year 2019 and the ensuing years.

We recommend the strict compliance and enforcement of collection of SET by
continuous information campaign and annual collection drive to increase available
resources to support the needs of schools in the municipality of Sulat.

We recommended and management agreed to :
(a) make careful planning of the LDRRMC to cater all projects, programs and
activities that are needed for disaster risk reduction and management, and revise
LDRRM Investment Plan, if needed, with proper authorization of the Sangguniang
Bayan.
(b) observe strict monitoring of the DRRM Funds and proper charging from it.

We recommended and management direct :
(a) the MDRRMO to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC
to this office, and further require the accounting and budget office units to assist in the
preparation of the said report to be certified by the Municipal Accountant;
(b) the MDRRMO and the Municipal accountant to include in the abovementioned
report the all available resources for LDRRM use such as unexpended prior year balances,
donations or grants received from the national agencies, local government units or foreign
sources, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) MSWDO to review meticulously all budget items included in her office to avoid
hidden MOOE items in the 20% Development Fund.
(b) Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate only those PPAs in the 20% Development
Fund which are allowed per DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommend that Management require the: (a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical Inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2017 and to maintain updated Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular 2002-003; (b) yearly conduct of physical count of PPE as mandated by law; (c) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular 2002-003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-001 Partially implemented (a) Municipal General Services Officer conducted physical count reports however made and submitted to Municipal Accountant by the GSO is incomplete as to the amounts. 2. PPELC and RPLC were initially started but not regularly updated.
observations.reason: Conducted physical count reports were made and submitted to Municipal. Accountant by the GSO however submitted reports are incomplete as to the amounts. PPELC abd RPLC were initially started but not regularly updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials and to officials without proper designation as disbursing officer and without proper bonding in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-002 Partially implemented. Current year’s findings includes the amount P22,000.00 made to and without the need for special disbursing officers bonding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from perfection thereof as required by COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for immediate review and communicating timely and relevant audit results to management promptly.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-03 Partially implemented. There were still lacking documents in various DVs immediately submit the lacking documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted. Stop the practice of paying without the required complete documents.
observations.reason: There were still lacking documents in various DVs submitted to the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management (a) Immediately craft a well-defined guidelines through an internal policy, on the Request Procurement, Receipt, Disposition and Monitoring of the Medicines and Medical supplies/equipment to ensure that accountabilities and safeguarding, including risk ownership are in place while in its custody. (b) The MHO, should strictly ensure the proper implementation of the policy. Non-compliance will be his sole responsibility/accountability, being the head of the department.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-04 Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management revert the accounts payable which are payable which are outstanding for two years already. Prepare also the necessary journal entries for the said reversion.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-05 Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should: (a) Utilize the facilities in accordance to the intended use and within the timeline of the LGU; (b) Return the excess funds in compliance with the BUB MOAs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-06 Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC Chairman to submit all perfected contracts with supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from perfection thereof as required by COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for immediate review and communicating timely and relevant audit results to management promptly.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016- Implemented

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the Land, Land Improvements and Buildings be traced to the historical cost and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipal should be supported either with an original or transfer certificate of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR Report on physical count of Land, Land Improvements and Buildings No.01 Partially Implemented
observations.reason: This finding is related to the historical cost could not be ascertained due to property management’s failure to present its proof of ownership and to reconcile its recorded valuation with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor with a difference of P5,390,927.57.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the:
(a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit a Report of the Physical Inventory of
the Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2017 and to maintain updated
Property Cards as prescribed by Section 119 & 124 of COA Circular 2002-003;
(b) yearly conduct of physical count of PPE as mandated by law;
(c) Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards,
Real Property Ledger Cards as prescribed by Section 119, 120 of COA Circular
2002-003.

We recommend that the management stop the practice of granting cash advances to
Officers and employees without proper designation or appointment as disbursing officer
and without proper bonding, otherwise the need for special disbursing officers should be
met by duly designating officers or employees as such in compliance with COA Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 17, 1997 and Section 101 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant/Responsible Officials
to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. Stop the practice of paying
without complete supporting documents.
Moving forward, the LCE should ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers are properly
authorized and supported with the required documents before its final approval for payment.
This is without prejudice to any disallowances that may be issued, if warranted.

We recommend that Management directs the Municipal Health Office in collaboration
with the Accounting Department to:
a. Immediately craft a well-defined guidelines through an internal policy, on the Request
for Procurement, Receipt, Disposition and Monitoring of the Medicines and Medical
supplies/equipment to ensure that accountabilities and safeguarding, including risk
ownership are in place while in its custody.
b. The MHO, should strictly ensure the proper implementation of the policy. Non-
compliance will be his sole responsibility/accountability, being the head of the
department.

We recommend that the Management revert the accounts payable which are more than
two years already. Prepare also the necessary journal entries for the said reversion.

We recommend that Management should:
a. Utilize the facilities in accordance to the intended use and within the timeline of the
LGU;
b. Return the excess funds in compliance with the BUB MOAs.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to: (a) maintain Property Ledger Card for each item of property; General Services Officer to: (b) prepare a complete and proper physical inventory report and the pertinent Property Card and reconcile the records with that of the Accounting Section to account for all properties of the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016; preparation.
observations.reason: Inventory taking is still on-going; Stock cards are being updated; Limited staff/ personnel could not be verified due to the absence of preparation.

observations.recommendation: a. Immediately close permanently the open dump site and start its rehabilitation. b. Direct the Local Solid Waste Management Board to intensify the information drive regarding the proper segregation of solid waste and the corresponding fines and penalties for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: Clearing and stripping off of waste layer & safe closure of dump-site rehabilitation completed. A letter and proposal seeking fund assistance from the NSWMC have been drafted. Study the possibility of availing fund assistance from the National Solid Waste Management Commission to augment fund for the procurement of land intended for a compliant final disposal site and Material Recovery Facility installation.
observations.reason: The LGU awaits the approval of its Solid Waste Management Plan as a requirement for the request for financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and/or the Municipal Budget Officer to stop charging expenditures that are operating and administrative in nature to 20% Development Fund. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Development Council include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) those programs and projects that are specifically included in the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 and any applicable issuance to ensure the attainment of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes for the constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Required documents already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should strictly observe the accounting, reporting and full disclosure requirements of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to the same regulation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016
observations.reason: To be reflected in the Notes to Financial Statement as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System and Technical Working Group to use clear and specific gender issues to be addressed in the preparation of LGU’s GAD Plan and include only PAPs that clearly address gender issues in the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 GAD Plan for CY 2017 were submitted in accordance with the prescribed format/guidelines.
observations.reason: This is a prior year finding and reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with Accounting Circular No. 2006-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise, withhold payments of any money due them in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 37 of PD 1445. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should stop granting additional cash advances to those employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015-003 98.32% out of total outstanding cash advances were already settled as of Demand letters were sent to them but no reply was received by the LGU.
observations.reason: Out of the 3 persons, the 2 were no longer with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and other concerned officers to strictly observe Executive Order 298 and NBC No. 486 dated March 26, 2003. Likewise, require the concerned officials and employees to refund immediately the excess amount of their present claims/reimbursements from the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR, CY 2015 All RCCs remit their collections within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC Chairman to always submit all perfected contracts with complete supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) working days from perfection thereof as required by COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for immediate review, thus, communicating audit results to management promptly.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015-6 This is a prior year finding and reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Stop immediately the delayed and partial remittance of collections on the part of collectors to the liquidating Officer. Enforce strictly Section 307 of RA 7160 to avoid accumulation of unremitted funds in their possession and avert risk of loss or misuse of the funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 All RCCs remit their collections within the prescribed period. Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents and to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Notice of Suspension/Disallowance may be subsequently issued by Team after a more thorough probe of the transactions is made based on the documents required to be submitted.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Required documents already submitted and complied.
observations.reason: Copies of documents were submitted to Audit Team within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: Require the General Services Officer to furnish the Audit Team copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports of the procured goods within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance/inspection thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Already complied. Copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports were furnished to Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the Land, Land Improvements and Buildings be reconciled to the historical cost and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipal should be supported either with an original or transfer certificate of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR Report on physical count of Real Property as well as Real Property Ledger Cards were already prepared. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipal should be supported either with an original or transfer certificate of title registered in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: This is a prior year finding; the parcels of land were already prepared but reconciliation to the Municipal Assessor records still in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) maintain Property Ledger Card for each item
of property; the General Services Officer to: (b) prepare a complete and proper physical
inventory report and the pertinent Property Card and reconcile the records with
that of the Accounting Section to account for all properties of the Municipality in
accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and to submit lacking documents to the Audit Team. Henceforth,
strictly observe the prescribed procurement process and comply with the
documentary requirements called for in some of the pertinent provisions of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of
Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer observed
strictly the existing Civil Service Commission (CSC) rules and regulations in
processing application for monetization of leave credits of officials and employees of
the Municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should refrain from
processing the payments for leave monetization with incomplete supporting
documents.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension
and/or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team all financial transactions with complete supporting
documents within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to facilitate review thereof.
